name: Phase Close -- tools/README + CHANGELOG Count Sync, NDEBT-002 Confirmation, Green Validator, v0.6.0 Release Gate

design_notes.release_gate_correction_v040_to_v060: MANDATORY CORRECTION (contract-time, F-019/F-026/F-027/F-028 precedent -- stale acceptance test corrected before implementation, not silently reworded at QA time). Both product_spec_004.md Sec 5 (F-030) and Sec 9 (Human Gates item 2) and feature_list_004.json's F-030 description ALL literally say 'v0.4.0 release gate' / 'v0.4.0 release human gate' -- but `git tag -l` (re-run at contract-authoring time) proves v0.4.0 ALREADY EXISTS (tagged 2026-07-08, per CHANGELOG.md [0.4.0] entry), and so do v0.4.1, v0.5.0, v0.5.1, v0.5.2, v0.5.3 -- five MORE releases shipped after the spec was drafted. `NIZAM.json` `framework.version` is currently `0.5.1` (confirmed by direct read). No `v0.6.0` tag exists yet (confirmed: `git tag -l 'v0.6.0'` is empty). Therefore the ACTUAL next-MINOR release gate this phase-close feature must record is **v0.6.0**, not v0.4.0. feature_list_004.json F-030 AT8 as literally written (`test "$(git tag -l 'v0.4.0' | wc -l)" -eq 0`) is FALSE on the current tree (v0.4.0 DOES exist, count=1, so the raw AT8 command as written would itself FAIL even though its intent -- 'no premature auto-cut tag for the phase-close release' -- is sound) -- CORRECTED here to assert the real gate: `test "$(git tag -l 'v0.6.0' | wc -l)" -eq 0` (no v0.6.0 tag exists; the pipeline records but does not execute the release). Every place this contract's verification or the CHANGELOG entries reference the release gate uses v0.6.0. This mirrors the exact contract-time-correction discipline the prompt's F-019/F-026/F-027/F-028 precedent set: the stale test/spec text is corrected here, in the contract, with the correction documented, rather than either (a) silently implementing the false v0.4.0 assertion (which would be a no-op non-gate, since v0.4.0 already exists and the raw test would fail) or (b) deferring the correction to QA time.
design_notes.no_framework_version_bump: This feature does NOT bump `NIZAM.json` `framework.version` (stays `0.5.1`). The v0.6.0 MINOR release is the HUMAN's release action (tag-cutting + version bump + changelog roll-up per methodology/06_release_train.md Sec 6, Release Mechanics Ownership), not something this pipeline feature performs. This is also load-bearing for C10's version-anchor sub-check (product_spec_004.md Sec 5 F-027, External Anchor Rule): C10 asserts every doc embedding the framework version (the guide's `<meta framework-version>` + footer) equals `NIZAM.json` `framework.version` -- if this feature bumped `NIZAM.json` alone without also re-synchronizing every anchor-doc, C10 would immediately regress from PASS to FAIL. Leaving `NIZAM.json` untouched keeps every existing anchor consistent and C10 green. Verified explicitly (verification entry 12).
design_notes.ndebt_002_already_resolved: docs/planning/DEBT.md was read in full before authoring this contract. NDEBT-002 is ALREADY present in the '## Resolved' section (dated 2026-07-10, resolution text: 'RESOLVED 2026-07-10, both parts: (a) F-025 reconciled qa_verdict.schema.json to an anyOf union ... (b) F-028 added validator check C11 ... Shipped in features 025 + 028 (commits bcb02d2, 0840094). validate.sh now at C1-C11, SUMMARY 11 passed.') -- this was done at the F-028 phase-close-adjacent commit, NOT deferred to this feature. feature_list_004.json F-030 AT5/AT6 ('NDEBT-002 moved to Resolved' / 'no longer in Open') therefore verify an ALREADY-SATISFIED end state, not an action this feature performs. Per AH-2 (detect before fix) and the scope guard's own allowance ('DEBT.md need not change'), this contract's scope explicitly does NOT include docs/planning/DEBT.md -- it is excluded from files_modify, and a non-goal + a dedicated verification entry (13) assert the file's diff against HEAD is empty (no re-move, no edit, no duplicate Resolved entry). AT5/AT6 are retained as READ-ONLY confirmation checks (they must already pass on the pre-implementation tree, and this contract's implementation must not cause them to regress) rather than as work items.
design_notes.current_tree_state_confirmed: Re-confirmed at contract-authoring time (matches the F-023..F-029 features' own closing claims), WITH ONE SELF-REFERENTIAL CAVEAT surfaced by rev0's rejection (see rev1_corrections): `bash tools/validate.sh` exits 0 with the literal last line `SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed` on the tree EXCLUDING this proposed contract document itself. Because C11 (added by F-028) validates every `.agent/contracts/*.json` including a proposed-but-not-yet-approved contract, the tree WITH `.agent/contracts/030.json` present is only `11 passed, 0 failed` once 030.json itself is schema-valid (i.e. once it carries both `files_create` and `files_modify` under `scope`, as this rev1 now does) -- rev0 omitted `files_create` and so the tree WITH rev0 present measured `SUMMARY: 10 passed, 1 failed`. `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- . ':(exclude).agent'` is EMPTY (clean tree, no stray uncommitted work from 023-029). `tools/README.md` frontmatter `version` is currently `0.2.0`, carries NO `change_log` key yet, and its body does NOT currently enumerate C1-C11 (it predates F-026/027/028's checks entirely -- it was last touched at framework genesis). `CHANGELOG.md`'s `## [Unreleased]` section is currently EMPTY (confirmed: exactly one blank line between the `## [Unreleased]` heading and the `## [0.5.1] - 2026-07-09` heading). This feature is therefore the FIRST feature to write real content into `tools/README.md`'s C-check description and into `CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased]` for phase 004.
design_notes.human_gates_recorded_not_executed: Per product_spec_004.md Sec 9 and the phase's 'no new consumer runtime behavior' discipline, this feature RECORDS (in tools/README.md prose and/or CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] framing) but does NOT EXECUTE any of: (1) the v0.6.0 tag/release itself (methodology/06_release_train.md Sec 6 assigns tag-cutting to the human/release-manager role), (2) GitHub Pages publishing for docs/guide/ (no .github/workflows/pages.yml or gh-pages.yml is created -- verification entry explicitly asserts their absence), (3) the phase-004 branch's PR merge. All three are noted as outstanding human gates. The MINOR classification (v0.6.0, not a MAJOR) is justified per methodology/06_release_train.md's semver rules: this phase adds only ADDITIVE validator checks (C9, C10, C11), an additive library (verify_lib.sh) and standard section, additive schema permissiveness (union/anyOf reconciliation -- strictly widens what validates, narrows nothing), and an additive CI job (e2e_bootstrap) -- with zero breaking runtime change and bootstrap.sh completely unmodified across the whole phase (F-023 through F-029 all confirmed this in their own non_goals).
design_notes.rev1_corrections: REVISION 1 of 3 (both Loop-1 gates rejected rev0 on two independently reproduced, localized defects; everything else -- the v0.4.0->v0.6.0 gate correction, the NDEBT-002 read-only no-op, NIZAM.json held at 0.5.1, the CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entries, the untracked-aware scope guard, C5 -- was confirmed sound and is UNCHANGED). FIX 1: `scope.files_create` was entirely absent from rev0 (only `files_modify` was present), which made 030.json itself schema-invalid against `schema/contract.schema.json` (`required: ["files_create", "files_modify"]`) -- and because C11 (this very phase's own dogfood check, added by F-028) validates every `.agent/contracts/*.json` including proposed contracts, the CURRENT tree with 030.json present actually reported `SUMMARY: 10 passed, 1 failed`, contradicting rev0's own `current_tree_state_confirmed` claim of `11 passed, 0 failed`. Nicely ironic: the phase's own closing contract was caught red-handed by the very check (C11) it is closing out. Fixed by adding `"files_create": []` (this feature creates no new files -- truthful and correct) to the `scope` object. FIX 2: rev0's `tools/README.md` plan claimed a body-only dated note satisfies C8 and 'matches existing module convention' -- both false. `tools/validate.sh --target` (AT2's mechanism) never invokes C8 (`check_c8_version_changelog` is repo-wide-only, confirmed by inspecting the `--target` dispatcher and validate.sh's own `--help` text), and C8's real implementation only accepts a frontmatter `change_log` entry or a literal CHANGELOG.md path mention -- reproduced live: a body-note-only bump + full sweep -> `[C8] FAIL`. `tools/SKILL.md` and `tools/interface.md` (the module's only other version-bumped docs) both in fact use frontmatter `change_log` arrays, not body notes. Fixed by switching `scope.files_modify[tools/README.md].purpose` to a frontmatter `change_log` entry (version 0.3.0, dated 2026-07-10) and adding verification entry 17 (below), which directly proves C8 passes for tools/README.md via the DEFAULT (non-`--target`) sweep -- closing the `missing_acceptance_coverage` gap the evaluator flagged, since AT2 structurally cannot exercise C8.

files_modify.purpose: Bump frontmatter `version` 0.2.0 -> 0.3.0 AND add a matching frontmatter `change_log` entry (`version: "0.3.0"`, `date: "2026-07-10"`, a `summary` naming the C1-C11 migration) to the file's own frontmatter block -- widening its existing 6-key frontmatter shape by adding the `change_log` array key, exactly matching this module's own precedent (`tools/SKILL.md` and `tools/interface.md`, the module's only other version-bumped docs, both already carry a frontmatter `change_log` array; neither uses a body note). This is the mechanism `tools/validate.sh`'s C8 (`check_c8_version_changelog`) actually accepts: a frontmatter `change_log` entry whose `version` equals the new version, OR a literal CHANGELOG.md path mention -- NOT prose in the document body, which C8 never reads (confirmed empirically: a body-note-only bump produces `[C8] FAIL` on the full sweep, and `validate.sh --target` does not invoke C8 at all, so AT2 alone cannot detect this). Separately from the frontmatter change_log entry, add a concise C1-C11 description in the document BODY: a table or list enumerating checks C1 through C11 by name (frontmatter schema, format, untagged-fence sweep, NIZAM.json index integrity, branding/endpoint leakage, bootstrap.sh sanity, module README presence, version-bump-vs-changelog, repo-wide path-resolution, single-source-of-truth consistency, dogfood schema validation) and stating the current `SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed` total -- this body content satisfies AT1's descriptive requirement, while the frontmatter `change_log` entry (not the body) is what satisfies C8. Does not alter the existing DD-4 section, the existing file table, or any other content outside these two additions.

Populate the currently-EMPTY `## [Unreleased]` section with phase-004 deliverable entries (Keep-a-Changelog `### Added` heading), each naming the REAL deliverable: (1) `tools/verify_lib.sh` (the five named sourced primitives) + the verification-authoring standard added to `methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md`; (2) validator checks C9 (repo-wide path-resolution), C10 (single-source-of-truth consistency), and C11 (dogfood schema validation against `.agent/` artifacts) -- naming the SUMMARY migration 8 -> 11 passed; (3) the reconciled `schema/qa_verdict.schema.json` `anyOf` union, the new `schema/contract_review.schema.json`, and `contract.schema.json`'s explicit `amendments[]` property (resolving NDEBT-002); (4) `tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh` (the hermetic end-to-end consumer-bootstrap harness) and its CI job in `.github/workflows/compliance.yml`. No other section of CHANGELOG.md (any dated `## [x.y.z]` entry) is touched.

non_goals: No edit to docs/planning/DEBT.md -- NDEBT-002 is ALREADY in the Resolved section (done at the F-025/F-028 close, commit e5b20f0 per the human's brief / 'bcb02d2, 0840094' per DEBT.md's own resolution text); this feature's AT5/AT6 verify that already-satisfied end state read-only and do not re-move or duplicate the entry (see design_notes.ndebt_002_already_resolved)

No edit to tools/validate.sh, tools/verify_lib.sh, tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh, any schema/*.json, .github/workflows/compliance.yml, or bootstrap.sh -- the validator stays at exactly C1-C11 / 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'; this feature is a documentation/changelog sync only

No bump to NIZAM.json framework.version (stays 0.5.1) and no edit to NIZAM.json at all -- the v0.6.0 bump is the human's release action, and C10's version-anchor sub-check requires every anchor doc to keep matching NIZAM.json as-is (see design_notes.no_framework_version_bump)
No git tag is cut (no v0.6.0, no v0.4.0 -- neither is created or deleted by this feature) and no push/release/publish action of any kind
No GitHub Pages workflow is created or enabled (no .github/workflows/pages.yml, no gh-pages.yml)
No edit to any immutable prior-feature artifact: no .agent/contracts/*.json (other than this feature's own 030.json), no .agent/qa/*.json, no .agent/evidence/*, no prior feature_list_004.json entries
No edit to CONTEXT.md, README.md, methodology/*, standard/*, registry/*, templates/*, docs/guide/index.html, or docs/architecture/* -- the scope is exactly tools/README.md + CHANGELOG.md
No re-litigation or re-fix of NDEBT-003, NDEBT-004, or NDEBT-005 (all correctly OPEN per DEBT.md, out of this feature's scope) and no edit to their entries
No implementation happens under this contract-proposal step -- this document is the PROPOSED contract only, per the current Loop-1 pre-code alignment stage

verification.acceptance_test: F-030 AT1: tools/README.md describes the C1-C11 set and the new total.
verification.command: grep -q 'C11' tools/README.md && grep -Eq '11 (checks|passed)' tools/README.md && echo PASS || { echo "tools/README.md missing C11 or the '11 checks/passed' total"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-030 AT2: tools/README.md version strictly bumped vs HEAD and still frontmatter-valid under --target (NOTE: --target does not exercise C8 -- see verification entry 17 for the direct C8 proof).
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_version_increased tools/README.md || { echo "tools/README.md version was not strictly increased vs HEAD"; exit 1; }; bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/README.md; rc=$?; test "$rc" -eq 0 || { echo "validate.sh --target tools/README.md exited $rc"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-030 AT3: CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] names the phase-004 deliverables by their real names (verify_lib.sh, contract_review.schema.json, the e2e harness).
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import re, sys
t = open('CHANGELOG.md', encoding='utf-8').read()
m = re.search(r'## \[Unreleased\](.*?)(\n## \[|\Z)', t, re.S)
assert m, 'no [Unreleased] section found'
s = m.group(1)
need = ['verify_lib.sh', 'contract_review.schema.json', 'e2e_bootstrap_test.sh']
missing = [n for n in need if n not in s]
sys.exit(1 if missing else 0)
" && echo PASS || { echo "CHANGELOG [Unreleased] missing one or more of: verify_lib.sh, contract_review.schema.json, e2e_bootstrap_test.sh"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-030 AT4: CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] names the new validator checks C9/C10/C11 and the count migration.
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import re, sys
t = open('CHANGELOG.md', encoding='utf-8').read()
m = re.search(r'## \[Unreleased\](.*?)(\n## \[|\Z)', t, re.S)
assert m, 'no [Unreleased] section found'
s = m.group(1)
sys.exit(0 if ('C9' in s and 'C10' in s and 'C11' in s) else 1)
" && echo PASS || { echo "CHANGELOG [Unreleased] missing C9/C10/C11"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-030 AT5 (READ-ONLY CONFIRMATION -- already satisfied pre-implementation, see design_notes.ndebt_002_already_resolved): NDEBT-002 is present in DEBT.md's Resolved section.
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import sys
t = open('docs/planning/DEBT.md', encoding='utf-8').read()
res = t.split('## Resolved')[-1]
sys.exit(0 if 'NDEBT-002' in res else 1)
" && echo PASS || { echo "NDEBT-002 not found in Resolved section"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-030 AT6 (READ-ONLY CONFIRMATION -- already satisfied): NDEBT-002 no longer appears in the Open section.
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import sys
t = open('docs/planning/DEBT.md', encoding='utf-8').read()
openpart = t.split('## Resolved')[0]
sys.exit(1 if 'NDEBT-002' in openpart else 0)
" && echo PASS || { echo "NDEBT-002 unexpectedly still present in the Open section"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-030 AT7: the final tree passes the compliance validator at the full C1-C11 count.
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -eq 0 || { echo "validate.sh exited $rc"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | tail -1 | grep -qx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed' || { echo "unexpected SUMMARY line: $(echo \"$out\" | grep '^SUMMARY:')"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-030 AT8 [CORRECTED v0.4.0 -> v0.6.0 per design_notes.release_gate_correction_v040_to_v060: v0.4.0 already exists as a shipped tag (2026-07-08) and four more MINOR/PATCH tags shipped after it (v0.4.1, v0.5.0, v0.5.1, v0.5.2, v0.5.3); NIZAM.json framework.version is 0.5.1; the actual next-MINOR release gate this feature records is v0.6.0, which does not yet exist]: the v0.6.0 release is a human gate and is NOT auto-cut by this feature.
verification.command: test "$(git tag -l 'v0.6.0' | wc -l)" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "a v0.6.0 tag unexpectedly exists -- this feature must not cut it"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-030 AT9: GitHub Pages / publishing not auto-enabled by this phase (no new Pages workflow).
verification.command: ! test -f .github/workflows/pages.yml && ! test -f .github/workflows/gh-pages.yml && echo PASS || { echo "a Pages workflow file was unexpectedly created"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-030 AT10: CHANGELOG.md introduces no branding/endpoint leakage (C5 covers the whole file, but this asserts it explicitly for the new [Unreleased] content).
verification.command: ! grep -Eqi 'nizamiq|\.svc|cluster\.local' CHANGELOG.md && echo PASS || { echo "CHANGELOG.md contains branding/endpoint leakage"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-030 AT11 (untracked-aware scope guard, per methodology/02 Sec 10 / verify_lib.sh precedent): only tools/README.md and CHANGELOG.md changed outside .agent/ -- docs/planning/DEBT.md is deliberately NOT in the allow-list (it must be unchanged, asserted separately in entry 13).
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'tools/README.md' 'CHANGELOG.md' && echo PASS || { echo "out-of-scope change(s) detected"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, per design_notes.no_framework_version_bump): NIZAM.json is byte-identical to HEAD -- framework.version stays 0.5.1, and every C10 version-anchor doc stays consistent with it.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- NIZAM.json && echo PASS || { echo "NIZAM.json was modified -- out of scope, and would risk a C10 version-anchor regression"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, per design_notes.ndebt_002_already_resolved): docs/planning/DEBT.md is byte-identical to HEAD -- this feature performs no DEBT.md edit (NDEBT-002 was already resolved at the F-025/F-028 close).
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- docs/planning/DEBT.md && echo PASS || { echo "docs/planning/DEBT.md was modified -- out of scope for this contract (NDEBT-002 already resolved)"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): tools/validate.sh, tools/verify_lib.sh, tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh, bootstrap.sh, .github/workflows/compliance.yml, and every schema/*.json are byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- tools/validate.sh tools/verify_lib.sh tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh bootstrap.sh .github/workflows/compliance.yml schema/ && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope immutable-for-this-feature file was modified"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- tools/validate.sh tools/verify_lib.sh tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh bootstrap.sh .github/workflows/compliance.yml schema/; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity, C1-C11 enumeration is REAL, not a bare substring hit): tools/README.md names every one of C1 through C11 individually (not merely 'C11' and '11 passed' in isolation, which AT1 alone would tolerate as a minimal substring match).
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import sys
t = open('tools/README.md', encoding='utf-8').read()
missing = [f'C{n}' for n in range(1, 12) if f'C{n}' not in t]
sys.exit(1 if missing else 0)
" && echo PASS || { echo "tools/README.md does not individually name every check C1-C11"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity, no pre-existing false-pass): re-confirm tools/README.md at HEAD (pre-implementation) does NOT already contain 'C11' or 'SUMMARY: 11', so AT1/AT7-style checks are proven non-vacuous rather than trivially already-true.
verification.command: ! git show HEAD:tools/README.md | grep -Eq 'C11|SUMMARY: 11' && echo PASS || { echo "tools/README.md at HEAD already mentions C11/SUMMARY: 11 -- AT1 would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (rev1, closes the missing_acceptance_coverage gap from .agent/qa/030-contract-review.json rev0: AT2's --target mode never invokes C8, so no prior entry directly proved C8 passes for tools/README.md's version bump): tools/README.md carries a frontmatter change_log entry for version 0.3.0, AND the DEFAULT (full, non---target) validate.sh sweep reports C8 passing on the final tree.
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import re, sys
import yaml
text = open('tools/README.md', encoding='utf-8').read()
lines = text.splitlines()
assert lines and lines[0].strip() == '---', 'no frontmatter fence found'
end = next(i for i in range(1, len(lines)) if lines[i].strip() == '---')
fm = yaml.safe_load('\n'.join(lines[1:end]))
assert isinstance(fm, dict), 'frontmatter did not parse as a mapping'
entries = fm.get('change_log') or []
assert isinstance(entries, list) and any(str(e.get('version')) == '0.3.0' for e in entries), 'no change_log entry for version 0.3.0 found in tools/README.md frontmatter'
print('frontmatter change_log 0.3.0 entry: CONFIRMED')
" || { echo "tools/README.md frontmatter change_log 0.3.0 entry missing"; exit 1; }; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -eq 0 || { echo "validate.sh exited $rc"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C8\] PASS' || { echo "C8 did not report PASS on the full sweep"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/030-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/030-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/030-verify-17.txt for the seventeenth -- the 'verification' array has exactly 17 entries, rev1). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
